data.title: Analysts predict that gold and silver may reach $5,000 and $90 respectively by 2026.
data.description: Spot gold hit a record high of $4,530.6 per ounce on Friday, while spot silver briefly touched a record high of $75.14. Kelvin Wong, senior market analyst at OANDA, said that since early December, momentum-driven and speculative traders have been driving the rise in gold and silver prices. Thin year-end liquidity, expectations of prolonged Fed rate cuts, a weaker dollar, and surging geopolitical risks have all contributed to pushing precious metals to new record highs. Gold could potentially move towards $5,000 per ounce in the first half of next year, while silver has the potential to reach around $90 per ounce. In addition, platinum and palladium have surged due to tight supply, tariff uncertainty, and rotating investment demand for gold. Platinum has risen approximately 165% year-to-date, and palladium over 90%. Jigar Trivedi, senior research analyst at Reliance Securities, said that platinum is supported by strong industrial demand, with US inventory holders replenishing their stocks amid sanctions-related concerns, which has helped maintain high prices. (Jinshi)

data.title: MGBX will launch LIT (Lighter) spot trading.
data.description: According to official news, MGBX will list LIT (Lighter) on December 26, 2025 at 18:00 (SGT). Spot trading deposit opening time: December 26, 2025 at 17:00 (SGT). Trading opening time: December 26, 2025 at 18:00 (SGT). Withdrawal opening time: To be determined. Lighter is a perpetual trading protocol that introduces a scalable, secure, transparent, non-custodial, and verifiable order book trading infrastructure into the Ethereum ecosystem.

data.description:

President Vladimir Putin allegedly announced the collaboration at a pre-New Year meeting of the State Council on Christmas. Putin said […]

data.title: On Coinbase, the price of BTC showed a negative premium of approximately -0.1% compared to several major Asian exchanges for one consecutive hour.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, the price of Bitcoin on Coinbase showed a negative premium of approximately -0.1% for about an hour today, compared to several other major Asian exchanges. This has now narrowed to -0.0632%. Furthermore, the Coinbase premium index has been in negative territory for 12 consecutive days since the 15th of this month. A negative premium on the Coinbase Bitcoin premium index indicates that the price of Bitcoin on Coinbase is lower than the global average. This usually reflects significant selling pressure in the US market, decreased investor risk appetite, increased market risk aversion, or capital outflows.

data.title: Trust Wallet hackers have transferred $4.25 million worth of stolen assets to CEX.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, Trust Wallet was hacked, affecting hundreds of users and resulting in the theft of over $6.77 million to date. The hackers have reportedly sent approximately $4.25 million worth of stolen assets to centralized exchanges (CEXs) such as ChangeNOW, FixedFloat, KuCoin, and HTX. Previously, CZ had stated that Trust Wallet would fully cover the losses.

data.title: A newly created wallet withdrew over 140,000 LINK tokens from Binance, worth approximately $1.73 million.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, a newly created wallet withdrew 140,019 LINK from Binance 20 minutes ago, worth approximately $1.73 million. In the past two days, this wallet has withdrawn a total of 469,437 LINK from Binance, worth $5.77 million.

data.title: Solstice: The de-anchoring of USX on the Solana chain is a secondary market liquidity issue; the underlying net asset value and custodial assets are unaffected.
data.description: Solstice stated on the X platform that the USX de-pegging on the Solana chain was due to a secondary market liquidity issue. The team and market makers are immediately working to resolve the problem and inject liquidity into the secondary market to ensure market stability. The net asset value of the underlying USX assets and the assets held in custody by Solstice are completely unaffected, and the collateralization ratio exceeds 100%. A supplementary certification report has been requested from a third party, and the team will release it immediately upon completion. It is understood that the stablecoin USX on the Solana chain experienced a brief de-pegging, with its price falling to $0.1 in the secondary market. Subsequently, after Solstice injected liquidity, the USX price recovered to $0.94.

data.title: Moody's Economist Warns of Fragile Economic Balance
data.description: According to Odaily, Moody's Chief Economist has issued a warning that while multiple interest rate cuts may occur in 2026, this should not be seen as a sign of prosperity. Instead, it reflects an economy in a 'fragile balance.' This unusual combination suggests a gradual and cautious approach to interest rates in the future, rather than an aggressive rate-cutting cycle. Data from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) indicates that only 64,000 jobs were added in November 2025. The agency noted that since April, there has been 'little net change' in employment numbers.

data.title: Market Sentiment Turns Bearish as Funding Rates Shift
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Coinglass indicates that the funding rates on major centralized and decentralized exchanges have shifted, signaling a bearish market sentiment. The funding rates for key cryptocurrencies are detailed in the accompanying chart.

Funding rates are fees set by cryptocurrency trading platforms to maintain the balance between contract prices and the prices of the underlying assets, typically applied to perpetual contracts. This mechanism facilitates the exchange of funds between long and short traders, without the trading platform collecting these fees. The purpose is to adjust the cost or profit of holding contracts, ensuring that contract prices remain close to the underlying asset prices.

A funding rate of 0.01% is considered the baseline rate. When the funding rate exceeds 0.01%, it indicates a generally bullish market sentiment. Conversely, when the funding rate falls below 0.005%, it suggests a bearish market outlook.

data.title: ZachXBT: A scammer impersonated a victim of the Trust Wallet hack to lure people into interacting with him.
data.description: Chain detective ZachXBT posted on the X platform that an account claiming to be a victim of a hacking attack has appeared in the community but is actually a scammer. This account attracts attention by posing as a woman and falsely claiming to be a victim of the Trust Wallet hack. The account exhibits several suspicious behaviors: changing its username 44 times, participating in multiple memecoin scams, and having only posted 234 messages since its creation in 2023. Furthermore, ZachXBT has been pre-blocked.

data.title: Moody's: Fed rate cuts are expected next year, but patience is needed.
data.description: Moody's chief economist warned that while multiple interest rate cuts are possible in 2026, this is not a sign of prosperity, but rather a sign that the economy is caught in a "fragile equilibrium." This unusual combination points to a gradual, cautious interest rate path rather than an aggressive rate-cutting cycle. Data from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) shows that only 64,000 jobs were added in November 2025, with the agency noting that the "net change" in employment figures since April has been "minimal." (Jinshi)
